MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference

ZAHIR NASERI, 209 5816096 Zahirnasri@yahoo.com Pr Per Petitioner

AMY P. FRENZEN, ESQ., Deputy City Attorney 415) 554-3885 amy.frenzen@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

PETITIONER ZAHIR NASERI'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate.

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER ZAHIR NASERI'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate. After hearing oral argment, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate (California Public Records Act ? Gov. Code 6250 Et Seq.)
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner's petition for writ of mandate is denied without prejudice. Petitioner was required to provide a memorandum of points and authorities that includes "a statement of facts, a concise statement of the law, evidence and arguments relied on, and a discussion of the statutes, cases, and textbooks cited in support of the position advanced." (CRC 3.1113(b).) In this case, petitioner merely filed a notice of hearing and it is unclear exactly what relief petitioner seeks. This Public Records Act petition for writ of mandate is not excepted from the memorandum requirement. (See CRC 3.1114(a).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/REQ)
